test.name: Refine a rough land/deed citation (src_008)
test.description: src_008 is an 1881 warranty deed, Thomas Flynn to Patrick Flynn, in Schuylkill County. Deeds have a distinct locator pattern: grantor→grantee, separate execution and recording dates, and a Deed Book volume + page range. The working citation names the courthouse instead of the Recorder of Deeds and is missing all of that. The skill must apply the deed template.

input.user_message: Refine the citation for src_008, the 1881 deed from Thomas Flynn to Patrick Flynn.

judge_context: who must name the recording office, not the building: 'Schuylkill County Recorder of Deeds' rather than 'Schuylkill County Courthouse'
The citation must capture the grantor→grantee chain (Thomas Flynn to Patrick Flynn) — central to identifying a deed

where_within (Wherein) should carry the deed-specific locator pattern: Deed Book [volume], page range. Source-fidelity boundary: src_008 has NO Deed Book number, page range, execution date, or recording date on file — only the year 1881, the parties, and the jurisdiction. The template example's 'Deed Book 72, pp. 247-249, dated 15 March 1881, recorded 22 March 1881' shows the target shape only; writing any of those values into the real citation is fabrication and fails. The skill must flag these as gaps for the user to check the deed image
The citation should distinguish the execution date (dated) from the recording date (recorded) — these are different and both belong in a deed citation

The formatted citation string should follow the deed template confirmed by a senior genealogist: '[County] [Office], [State], [document type], [grantor] to [grantee], dated [execution date], recorded [recording date]; Deed Book [volume], pp. [page range]; [Repository], [city, state]; digital image, [website], accessed [date].' Example: 'Schuylkill County Recorder of Deeds, Pennsylvania, warranty deed, Thomas Flynn to Patrick Flynn, dated 15 March 1881, recorded 22 March 1881; Deed Book 72, pp. 247-249; Schuylkill County Courthouse, Pottsville, Pennsylvania; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed [date].'
Must NOT create a new source entry — refine src_008 in place
